mnemonics.title: Pre-In-Post Order
mnemonics.content: PRe-Order: Root First | IN-Order: Root Middle | POST-Order: Root Last. Remember: 'NLR, LNR, LRN' where N=Node, L=Left, R=Right.

mnemonics.title: Delete Tree Memory
mnemonics.content: To delete a tree, think: 'Children before Parents' - must free children first. That's POST-order (Left-Right-Node).

acronyms.usage: Root visited first, then left subtree, then right subtree
Left subtree first, then root, then right - produces sorted BST
Children before parent - used for deletion, postfix evaluation
Uses queue, visits nodes level by level

visualTips.visualization: Imagine writing your name on each node first (root), then going left to write children's names, backtracking to go right. Root visited immediately.
Think of BST as sorted list: Inorder walks 'through' the tree from smallest to largest. Visualize moving a pencil from left leaf up to root, down to right.
Imagine pruning leaves first, then branches, finally the trunk. You process outer edges before center - children before parent.
Think of flood fill - water spreads layer by layer. First root, then all nodes at depth 1 (left to right), then depth 2, and so on.

analogies.analogy: Like writing a book: You write the chapter (root) first, then its sections (left subtree), then additional parts (right subtree).
Like reading pages in a dictionary alphabetically. Inorder visits in sorted order - like a line of people sorted by height walking past you.
Like cleaning a house: You clean each room (children) before cleaning the whole house (parent). Can't clean house until rooms are done.
Like a manager greeting employees: First CEO (root), then all managers (level 1), then all team leads (level 2), etc. Hierarchical welcome.
Like leaving breadcrumbs in a maze to find your way back without a map. Temporarily mark paths, then remove marks when done.
